skill.name: vercel-deployment
skill.description: Expert knowledge for deploying to Vercel with Next.js. Use when: vercel, deploy, deployment, hosting, production.
skill.summary: Expert knowledge for deploying Next.js applications to Vercel with best practices for environment configuration, edge/serverless functions, and build optimization.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill provides expert knowledge for deploying Next.js applications to Vercel. All static scanner findings were evaluated as false positives: the backticks flagged are markdown inline code formatting, not shell execution; the cryptographic and network flags are common technical terms used in their legitimate context (production deployment, edge functions). No security risks identified.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged line 79 backticks as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are markdown inline code fences referencing related skills, not shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are used in markdown for inline code formatting to reference skill names 'nextjs-app-router' and 'supabase-backend' - standard markdown syntax, not shell execution.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Common Terms Misidentified
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'production', 'Edge', and 'serverless' as security concerns. These are standard deployment terminology used appropriately in context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: 'production' describes deployment environment, 'Edge vs Serverless' discusses computing runtimes - all legitimate technical terminology with no security implications.

content.value_statement: This skill provides expert guidance for deploying Next.js applications to Vercel, covering environment configuration, edge and serverless functions, build optimization, and common deployment pitfalls.
actual_capabilities: Configure environment variables for dev, preview, and production environments
Choose between Edge and Serverless runtimes for API routes
Optimize builds to reduce cold starts and bundle size
Set up preview deployments for testing before production
Configure monitoring with analytics and error tracking
Avoid common pitfalls like exposing secrets in NEXT_PUBLIC_ variables
limitations: This skill does not include actual deployment scripts or CLI commands
Does not provide account-specific configuration for Vercel teams
Does not cover deployment to alternative hosting platforms
Requires basic understanding of Next.js App Router

use_cases.title: First-time Vercel deployment
use_cases.description: New developers learning how to deploy their first Next.js application to production
use_cases.target_user: Junior developers new to Vercel deployment

use_cases.title: Optimizing existing deployments
use_cases.description: Developers looking to improve performance of their Vercel-hosted applications
use_cases.target_user: Mid-level developers with existing Vercel projects

use_cases.title: Troubleshooting deployment issues
use_cases.description: Developers debugging cold start issues, environment variable problems, or function timeouts

prompt_templates.title: Basic deployment question
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I deploy my Next.js app to Vercel?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to understand the basic deployment process

prompt_templates.title: Environment configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: What environment variables do I need to set for my Vercel production deployment?
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to configure secrets for production

prompt_templates.title: Runtime selection
prompt_templates.prompt: When should I use Edge functions vs Serverless functions on Vercel?
prompt_templates.scenario: User is deciding which runtime to use for their API routes

prompt_templates.title: Build optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: How can I optimize my Vercel build to reduce cold start times and bundle size?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to improve deployment performance

output_examples.input: How do I deploy my Next.js app to Vercel?
output: To deploy to Vercel, you can use the Vercel CLI or connect your Git repository.
1. Using CLI: Run 'vercel' in your project root
2. Using Git: Push to GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket and import in Vercel dashboard
Key considerations: Configure environment variables, set up preview deployments for testing, and monitor with Vercel Analytics

output_examples.input: What are common mistakes to avoid when deploying to Vercel?
output: - Using NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix for secrets (exposes them to browser)
- Using production database for preview deployments
- Using Node.js APIs in Edge runtime that are not supported
- Missing environment variables at runtime that were present at build time

best_practices: Always use separate databases for preview and production environments to prevent data corruption
Use NEXT_PUBLIC_ only for values that should be visible in the browser
Configure proper caching headers to avoid serving stale content after deployments
anti_patterns: Storing API keys or secrets in NEXT_PUBLIC_ environment variables
Using the same database connection string for all environments
Disabling build cache to 'ensure fresh builds' (hurts performance)

faq.question: Do I need to install the Vercel CLI to deploy?
faq.answer: No. You can deploy directly from GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket by importing your repository in the Vercel dashboard. The CLI is optional but useful for local development.

faq.question: What is the difference between Edge and Serverless functions?
faq.answer: Edge functions run on edge networks closer to users for lower latency but have limited Node.js APIs. Serverless functions have full Node.js support but may have colder starts.

faq.question: Can I use environment variables at build time vs runtime?
faq.answer: Environment variables prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_ are embedded at build time. Other variables are available at runtime. This affects when you can change values without rebuilding.

faq.question: How do preview deployments work?
faq.answer: Preview deployments are automatically created for each pull request. They use their own environment and URL, allowing you to test changes before merging to production.

faq.question: What causes cold starts on Vercel?
faq.answer: Cold starts occur when a serverless function hasn't been invoked recently and needs to initialize. Larger functions and complex dependencies increase cold start time.

faq.question: Can I deploy non-Next.js projects to Vercel?
faq.answer: Yes, Vercel supports many frameworks including React, Vue, Svelte, and static sites. However, this skill focuses specifically on Next.js deployment patterns.
